children.heading: .—Administrator
children.label: 1 .—Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of General Services.

children.heading: .—Lighting system
children.label: 2 .—Lighting system
children.text: The term “lighting system” means the elements required to maintain a desired light level, including lamps, light fixtures, fixture distribution, sensors and control technologies, interior design elements, and daylighting sources.

children.label: A
children.text: procure the most life-cycle cost effective and energy efficient lighting systems; and

children.label: B
children.text: ensure that procurements after the date of enactment of the BRIGHT Act of lighting systems or the individual components of lighting systems maximize life-cycle cost effectiveness and energy efficiency.

children.heading: .—Use
children.label: 2 .—Use
children.text: Each public building constructed, altered, acquired, or leased by the Administrator shall be equipped, to the maximum extent practicable as determined by the Administrator, with the most life-cycle cost effective and energy efficient lighting systems for each application.

children.heading: .—Maintenance of Public Buildings
children.label: c .—Maintenance of Public Buildings
children.text: Each individual component of a lighting system, including a lamp or fixture, that is replaced by the Administrator in the normal course of maintenance of public buildings shall be replaced, to the maximum extent practicable, with the most life-cycle cost effective and energy efficient lighting system possible for the application.

children.heading: .—Contracting options
children.label: 1 .—Contracting options
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Administrator shall consider appropriate contracting options for the procurement of the most life-cycle cost effective and energy efficient lighting systems.

children.label: A
children.text: the compatibility of the lighting system with existing equipment, including consideration of a cost effective retrofit;

children.label: B
children.text: whether procurement and use of the lighting system could result in interference with productivity;

children.label: C
children.text: the aesthetics relating to the use of the lighting system; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other factors as the Administrator determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: .—Life-Cycle Cost Effective
children.label: e .—Life-Cycle Cost Effective
children.text: The Administrator shall use the procedures and methods established under section 544(a) of the National Energy Conservation Policy Act () in determining whether a lighting system is life-cycle cost effective.42 U.S.C. 8254(a)

the lighting system or the individual components of the lighting system are certified under the Energy Star program established by section 324A of the Energy Policy and Conservation Act ();42 U.S.C. 6294a

in the case of all light-emitting diode (LED) luminaires, lamps, and systems whose efficacy (lumens per watt) and Color Rendering Index (CRI) meet the Department of Energy requirements for minimum luminaire efficacy and CRI for the Energy Star certification, as verified by an independent third-party testing laboratory that the Administrator and the Secretary of Energy determine conducts its tests according to the procedures and recommendations of the Illuminating Engineering Society of North America, even if the luminaires, lamps, and systems have not received such certification; or
the Administrator and the Secretary of Energy have otherwise determined that the lighting system is energy efficient.

children.heading: .—Additional Energy Efficient Lighting Designations
children.label: g .—Additional Energy Efficient Lighting Designations
children.text: The Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency and the Secretary of Energy shall give priority to establishing Energy Star performance criteria or Federal Energy Management Program designations for additional lighting product categories that are appropriate for procurement and use in public buildings.

children.heading: .—Guidelines
children.label: h .—Guidelines
children.text: The Administrator shall develop guidelines for the procurement and use of energy efficient lighting technologies that contain mercury in child care centers in public buildings.

children.heading: .—Applicability of Buy American Act
children.label: i .—Applicability of Buy American Act
children.text: Acquisitions carried out pursuant to this section shall be subject to the requirements of the Buy American Act ( et seq.).141 U.S.C. 10cSee References in Text note below.1
